task_description:
# PocketBase v0.31.0 Go Migration with Seed Data

## Goal
Build a custom Go PocketBase v0.31.0 binary that ships exactly ONE reversible Go migration which creates two collections (`categories`, `articles`) and seeds them with deterministic initial data. The down step must remove both collections and the seeded records.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Project path: /home/user/myproject
- Binary path: /home/user/myproject/app
- Build command: `go build -o /home/user/myproject/app .` (executed in /home/user/myproject)
- Migrate up command: `/home/user/myproject/app migrate up`
- Migrate down command: `/home/user/myproject/app migrate down 1`
- Serve command: `/home/user/myproject/app serve --http=0.0.0.0:8090`
- Port: 8090
- After `./app migrate up`:
  - `GET http://localhost:8090/api/collections/categories/records` returns HTTP 200 with `totalItems = 3` and the `items[].name` values are exactly the set {`Tech`, `Life`, `News`} (order not enforced).
  - `GET http://localhost:8090/api/collections/articles/records` returns HTTP 200 with `totalItems = 6` and every `items[].category` value equals the `id` of one of the three category records.
  - Each article record contains non-empty `title` and `body` string fields.
  - The `category` field on `articles` is a single relation to `categories`, required, with cascade delete behavior.
  - The `name` field on `categories` is unique.
- After `./app migrate down 1` (run once after a successful up):
  - Both `categories` and `articles` collections are absent: `GET /api/collections/categories/records` and `GET /api/collections/articles/records` both return HTTP 404.
- After running `./app migrate up` again following the down:
  - The same 3 categories (`Tech`, `Life`, `News`) and exactly 6 articles are restored deterministically.
task_integrations: - PocketBase (target library; v0.31.0 used as a Go module)
truth:
# Verification Plan

## Setup
1. cd /home/user/myproject
2. Ensure no previous pb_data directory leaks into the test by removing it if present: `rm -rf /home/user/myproject/pb_data`
3. Build the binary: `go build -o /home/user/myproject/app .`
4. Bootstrap a superuser non-interactively (used only so the binary is healthy; REST endpoints under verification do not require auth because list/view rules are public for these collections): `/home/user/myproject/app superuser upsert admin@example.com Admin123! || true`

## Verification Steps
1. Run `./app migrate up` and confirm it exits with status 0 and prints at least one applied migration line.
2. Start `./app serve --http=0.0.0.0:8090` in the background and wait for the HTTP port to become available (poll for up to 30 seconds).
3. GET http://localhost:8090/api/collections/categories/records
   - Expected: HTTP 200, JSON body with `totalItems == 3`, and the set of `items[].name` equals {`Tech`, `Life`, `News`} (order independent).
   - Capture the three returned ids as `cat_ids`.
4. GET http://localhost:8090/api/collections/articles/records?perPage=50
   - Expected: HTTP 200, JSON body with `totalItems == 6`.
   - Every `items[].category` value MUST appear in `cat_ids`.
   - Every `items[].title` and `items[].body` MUST be non-empty strings.
5. GET http://localhost:8090/api/collections/categories
   - Expected: response describes a collection with a field named `name` whose `unique` attribute is true.
6. GET http://localhost:8090/api/collections/articles
   - Expected: response describes a `category` field of type `relation` with `required = true`, `maxSelect = 1`, `cascadeDelete = true`, and `collectionId` matching the categories collection id.
7. Stop the serve process.
8. Run `./app migrate down 1` and confirm exit status 0.
9. Restart `./app serve --http=0.0.0.0:8090` and wait for the port.
10. GET http://localhost:8090/api/collections/categories/records and GET http://localhost:8090/api/collections/articles/records
    - Expected: Both endpoints return HTTP 404 (collections removed).
11. Stop the serve process.
12. Run `./app migrate up` again and confirm exit status 0.
13. Restart serve, then repeat steps 3 and 4.
    - Expected: Same 3 category names and exactly 6 articles, each linked to one of the three new category ids.
14. Stop the serve process.
